pre_game.message: Hey there, I'm E. It’s a bit intense in here with everyone waiting for the game to start, isn't it? How are you holding up?
Hey E, I'm D. It's definitely a bit nerve-wracking, but I'm just trying to stay focused on the goal. How are you feeling about all this?
I'm feeling pretty determined, honestly. I've got people back home I really want to help out, so I'm just trying to stay sharp.
I hear you. It’s the same for me—having people counting on you definitely keeps the pressure on. Have you thought about how you’re going to play this?
I’m leaning towards cooperation if it keeps the pot high, but I’ve got to stay flexible since I need that win. What’s your take—trust everyone or watch your back?
I’m thinking a bit of both. I’d love to trust everyone so we can maximize the prize, but I have to keep my eyes open—I can't risk losing out when so many people are counting on me.
I completely get that; it's hard to fully let your guard down when the stakes are this high for the people we care about.
Definitely. It’s good to know we’re on the same page about why we’re here. What do you do back home when you’re not competing for prize pools?
I work as a delivery driver, lots of hours on the road which gives me plenty of time to think. What about you, what’s your day-to-day like?
I work at a hardware store, mostly stocking and helping folks out; it’s steady, but I really need this win to help my family out properly.
I respect that, steady work is good but it doesn't always get you ahead. Let's try to look out for each other in there so we both get what our families need. Good luck!

post_game.message: I'm really glad we all took our fair share of $10,000. It was a pleasure playing with you!
It was a pleasure playing with you as well! I'm glad we could both walk away with a fair share.
